name: press_pickups_embargo_window
definition: Count of published or syndicated stories citing your launch between embargo lift and +48 hours, excluding paid/sponsored placements.
target_hint: Cold (0 relationships): 0–1 realistic. Warm intro + strong hook: 1–3. Funding + tier-1 relationships: 3–8. Do not plan around TechCrunch unless you have a prior relationship.

name: press_pitch_reply_rate
definition: Reporters who reply (any tone) divided by personalized pitches sent (target 5).
target_hint: Cold list: 10–30% reply is good. 0% after 5 beat-matched pitches → hook or proof problem, not volume problem.

name: press_embargo_compliance
definition: Boolean — did all live stories publish at or after embargo lift with no founder-caused leaks?
target_hint: Must be 100%. One early leak can zero out coordinated coverage.

name: press_referral_sessions_72h
definition: Sessions with utm_source=press or referrer from target outlet domains in 72h post-embargo.
target_hint: Trade press: 200–2k sessions per pickup. Tier-1 tech: 1k–15k depending on story placement. Zero sessions with a pickup → wrong audience or weak CTA in story.

name: press_to_signup_rate
definition: Signups from press-referred sessions divided by press-referred sessions in launch week.
target_hint: 5–12% for strong message match on LP. <3% means landing page doesn't match story angle.

name: press_kit_download_or_open
definition: Reporters who confirm receiving/opening press kit PDF (track via link or direct reply).
target_hint: ≥3 of 5 pitched reporters should acknowledge kit. If 0 opens, attachment/link delivery is broken.

name: press_follow_up_sla_met
definition: Percentage of reporter replies answered within 24 business hours during pitch and launch window.
target_hint: Target 100% during T-7→D+2 window. One missed fact-check call can distort the published story.

name: press_founders_quote_used_verbatim
definition: Boolean — did ≥1 pickup use your pre-approved founder quote without hostile reframing?
target_hint: Pre-approved ≤60-word quotes get used ~70% of the time when story runs. Long or jargon quotes get rewritten.
